title: Windows Update File Not Found
shortDescription: Windows Update could not find a required file during the update process.
fullDescription: Error 0x80070002 (ERROR_FILE_NOT_FOUND) occurs when Windows Update attempts to access a file that is missing or has been moved. This commonly happens when the Windows Update cache is corrupted, when system files are damaged, or when a previous update was interrupted and left partial files behind.

causes.cause: Previous update interrupted or failed partially
Third-party software interfering with update files

symptoms: Windows Update fails with error 0x80070002
Update downloads but fails to install
Windows Update stuck checking for updates
Specific KB update repeatedly fails

fixSteps.title: Run Windows Update Troubleshooter
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Update & Security > Troubleshoot > Additional troubleshooters > Windows Update > Run the troubleshooter.

fixSteps.title: Clear Windows Update cache
fixSteps.instruction: Open elevated Command Prompt. Run: net stop wuauserv && net stop cryptSvc && net stop bits && ren C:\Windows\SoftwareDistribution SoftwareDistribution.old && ren C:\Windows\System32\catroot2 catroot2.old && net start wuauserv && net start cryptSvc && net start bits

fixSteps.title: Run SFC and DISM
fixSteps.instruction: Open elevated Command Prompt: sfc /scannow then DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth

fixSteps.title: Manually download and install the update
fixSteps.instruction: Go to the Microsoft Update Catalog (catalog.update.microsoft.com), search for the KB number, download the standalone package, and install manually.

whenToCallPro: If clearing the cache and running DISM do not resolve the issue, and manual installation also fails.

faq.q: Is it safe to delete the SoftwareDistribution folder?
faq.a: Yes. Renaming or deleting SoftwareDistribution forces Windows Update to recreate it with fresh data. No personal files are affected.

faq.q: Can I skip this update?
faq.a: You can temporarily hide the update, but security updates should be installed. Try manual installation from the Microsoft Update Catalog.

faq.q: Why does this keep happening?
faq.a: Recurring failures often indicate underlying system file corruption. Running SFC and DISM thoroughly usually breaks the cycle.

title: Windows Update Access Denied
shortDescription: Windows Update does not have the required permissions to install the update.
fullDescription: Error 0x80070005 (E_ACCESSDENIED) occurs when Windows Update lacks the necessary permissions to access files, registry keys, or services needed for the update. This can happen due to corrupted user profiles, restrictive security policies, antivirus interference, or corrupted Windows Update components.

causes.cause: Windows Update service running with incorrect permissions

symptoms: Windows Update fails with Access Denied error
Updates download but fail during installation phase
Store apps fail to update with the same error
Windows Activation fails with 0x80070005

fixSteps.title: Run Windows Update Troubleshooter
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Update & Security > Troubleshoot > Windows Update.

fixSteps.title: Reset Windows Update components
fixSteps.instruction: Open elevated Command Prompt and run: net stop wuauserv && net stop cryptSvc && net stop bits && net stop msiserver && ren C:\Windows\SoftwareDistribution SoftwareDistribution.old && ren C:\Windows\System32\catroot2 catroot2.old && net start wuauserv && net start cryptSvc && net start bits && net start msiserver

fixSteps.title: Temporarily disable antivirus
fixSteps.instruction: Disable third-party antivirus protection temporarily and retry the update.

fixSteps.title: Grant full control to update folders
fixSteps.instruction: Open elevated Command Prompt: icacls C:\Windows\SoftwareDistribution /grant administrators:F /t && icacls C:\Windows\System32\catroot2 /grant administrators:F /t

whenToCallPro: If the access denied error persists after resetting components and adjusting permissions, there may be deeper permission issues requiring professional repair.

faq.q: Can antivirus cause this error?
faq.a: Yes. Antivirus software with real-time protection can block Windows Update from accessing or modifying system files. Temporarily disabling it often resolves the issue.

faq.q: Does this affect Windows Activation too?
faq.a: Yes. The same error code can appear during Windows Activation if the licensing service lacks necessary permissions.

faq.q: Is my computer secure if updates fail?
faq.a: No. Failed security updates leave your system vulnerable. Resolve this error promptly and install pending security updates.

title: Invalid Data in Update Package
shortDescription: Windows Update encountered invalid data during the update process.
fullDescription: Error 0x8007000d (ERROR_INVALID_DATA) occurs when Windows Update detects corrupted or invalid data in the update package or component store. This frequently happens after incomplete downloads, corrupted Windows component store, or registry issues.
symptoms: Windows Update fails with error 0x8007000d
DISM reports errors about the component store
Feature updates fail repeatedly
In-place upgrade fails

fixSteps.title: Run DISM to repair component store
fixSteps.instruction: Open elevated Command Prompt: DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth. If this fails, use Windows installation media: DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth /Source:D:\Sources\install.wim

fixSteps.title: Run SFC
fixSteps.instruction: After DISM completes, run: sfc /scannow

fixSteps.title: Clear update cache and retry
fixSteps.instruction: Stop Windows Update services, delete contents of C:\Windows\SoftwareDistribution\Download, restart services.

fixSteps.title: Use Media Creation Tool
fixSteps.instruction: Download the Windows Media Creation Tool from Microsoft. Select Upgrade this PC now to perform an in-place upgrade that repairs the component store.

whenToCallPro: If DISM cannot repair the component store and in-place upgrade also fails.

faq.q: What is the component store?
faq.a: The Windows component store (WinSxS folder) contains files needed for Windows operation and updates. When it becomes corrupted, updates fail.

faq.q: Will an in-place upgrade erase my files?
faq.a: No. An in-place upgrade using the Media Creation Tool repairs Windows while preserving your files, applications, and settings.

faq.q: Can I delete the WinSxS folder to fix this?
faq.a: Absolutely not. The WinSxS folder is essential to Windows. Deleting it will break the system. Use DISM to repair it properly.

title: Invalid Parameter in Update Operation
shortDescription: An invalid parameter was passed to a Windows Update function.
fullDescription: Error 0x80070057 (E_INVALIDARG) occurs when Windows Update receives an invalid parameter during its operation. This is a generic error that can stem from corrupted update components, incorrect regional settings, problematic registry entries, or disk partition issues during feature updates.

causes.cause: Incorrect decimal symbol in regional settings
Disk partition issues during feature update

symptoms: Windows Update fails with error 0x80070057
Feature updates fail during installation
Backup operations fail with the same error
Windows installation fails with invalid parameter

fixSteps.title: Check regional settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Control Panel > Region > Additional settings. Ensure the Decimal symbol is set to a period (.) not a comma.

fixSteps.title: Reset Windows Update components
fixSteps.instruction: Run the Windows Update Troubleshooter, then clear the SoftwareDistribution folder as described in standard WU reset steps.

fixSteps.title: Run DISM and SFC
fixSteps.instruction: Open elevated Command Prompt: DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth then sfc /scannow

fixSteps.title: Modify update registry entry
fixSteps.instruction: Open Registry Editor. Navigate to HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\WindowsUpdate\UX\Settings. Delete or rename the IsConvergedUpdateStackEnabled value if it exists.

whenToCallPro: If the error occurs during Windows installation or feature updates and standard fixes do not help.

faq.q: Why does the decimal symbol matter?
faq.a: Some Windows Update components parse version numbers and sizes using the system's regional decimal symbol. A comma instead of a period can cause parsing failures.

faq.q: Can this affect Windows installation?
faq.a: Yes. During clean installs or feature updates, this error can occur due to partition configuration issues or invalid setup parameters.

faq.q: Is this error dangerous?
faq.a: The error itself is not dangerous, but failing updates leave your system without security patches. Resolve it to keep Windows updated.

title: Windows Update Download Failure
shortDescription: Windows Update failed to download the update package from Microsoft servers.
fullDescription: Error 0x80240034 (WU_E_DOWNLOAD_FAILED) indicates that Windows Update was unable to download one or more update packages. This can be caused by network connectivity issues, proxy or firewall blocking, corrupted update cache, or server-side problems.

causes.cause: Network connectivity issues or unstable connection
Firewall or proxy blocking Windows Update servers

symptoms: Update download progress stops or fails
Error 0x80240034 appears in update history
Multiple updates fail to download simultaneously
Download progress resets repeatedly

fixSteps.title: Check network connectivity
fixSteps.instruction: Test internet connection by opening a browser. Try switching between Wi-Fi and Ethernet. Reset router if needed.

fixSteps.title: Reset Windows Update cache
fixSteps.instruction: Stop WU services, clear SoftwareDistribution\Download folder, restart services.

fixSteps.title: Temporarily disable firewall/VPN
fixSteps.instruction: Disable third-party firewall and VPN temporarily. Retry the update.

fixSteps.title: Download update manually
fixSteps.instruction: Visit catalog.update.microsoft.com, search for the KB number, download and install the standalone package.

whenToCallPro: If the issue is related to corporate network/proxy configuration that you cannot modify.

faq.q: Can a VPN cause this error?
faq.a: Yes. VPNs can interfere with Windows Update downloads by routing traffic through servers that have poor connectivity to Microsoft's CDN.

faq.q: Should I wait and try later?
faq.a: If other internet services work fine, try resetting the cache first. If nothing works, wait 24 hours as Microsoft servers may have temporary issues.

faq.q: Can I use a metered connection?
faq.a: By default, Windows does not download updates over metered connections. Unmark the connection as metered in Network Settings if applicable.

title: Windows Update HTTP 503 Service Unavailable
shortDescription: The Windows Update server returned a 503 (Service Unavailable) error.
fullDescription: Error 0x80244022 (WU_E_PT_HTTP_STATUS_SERVICE_UNAVAIL) indicates that Windows Update received an HTTP 503 response from Microsoft's update servers, meaning the service is temporarily overloaded or undergoing maintenance.
symptoms: Windows Update fails with 0x80244022
All updates fail at the same time
Error occurs primarily on Patch Tuesday
Update check itself fails

fixSteps.title: Wait and retry
fixSteps.instruction: This error is usually temporary. Wait 30 minutes to a few hours and try again. It is common immediately after Patch Tuesday.

fixSteps.title: Flush DNS cache
fixSteps.instruction: Open elevated Command Prompt: ipconfig /flushdns

fixSteps.title: Reset network settings
fixSteps.instruction: Open elevated Command Prompt: netsh winsock reset && netsh int ip reset. Restart the computer.

fixSteps.title: Change DNS servers
fixSteps.instruction: Change DNS to Google (8.8.8.8, 8.8.4.4) or Cloudflare (1.1.1.1, 1.0.0.1) in network adapter settings.

whenToCallPro: In enterprise environments, this may indicate WSUS server issues requiring IT admin intervention.

faq.q: Is this a problem with my computer?
faq.a: Usually not. This error indicates the Microsoft update server is temporarily overloaded. It typically resolves itself within hours.

faq.q: Why does this happen on Patch Tuesday?
faq.a: Patch Tuesday (second Tuesday of each month) creates a surge of update requests worldwide, sometimes overwhelming Microsoft's servers.

faq.q: Can my ISP cause this?
faq.a: Rarely. However, ISP-level DNS issues or network problems can occasionally contribute to connectivity failures with update servers.

title: Component Store Corruption
shortDescription: The Windows component store (WinSxS) is corrupted, preventing update installation.
fullDescription: Error 0x80073712 (ERROR_SXS_COMPONENT_STORE_CORRUPT) indicates that the Windows side-by-side component store has become corrupted. This store contains all Windows components and is essential for updates. Corruption can result from interrupted updates, disk errors, or system file corruption.

causes.cause: Component store corruption from interrupted update
Third-party cleanup tool removed necessary components
Failed feature update left partial components

symptoms: Windows Update fails with 0x80073712
DISM reports component store corruption
Multiple unrelated updates all fail
SFC finds errors it cannot repair

fixSteps.title: Run DISM RestoreHealth
fixSteps.instruction: Open elevated Command Prompt: DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth. If it fails, use installation media: DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth /Source:D:\Sources\install.wim

fixSteps.title: Run SFC
fixSteps.instruction: After DISM: sfc /scannow

fixSteps.title: Run StartComponentCleanup
fixSteps.instruction: Open elevated Command Prompt: DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /StartComponentCleanup

fixSteps.title: In-place upgrade repair
fixSteps.instruction: Download Windows Media Creation Tool, select Upgrade this PC now. This replaces corrupted components while keeping files.

whenToCallPro: If DISM with installation media source and in-place upgrade both fail to repair the component store.

faq.q: Can cleanup tools cause this?
faq.a: Yes. Third-party cleanup tools (like CCleaner) that delete files from the WinSxS folder can corrupt the component store. Never manually clean WinSxS.

faq.q: Will DISM always fix it?
faq.a: DISM can repair most component store corruption, especially when pointed at a clean Windows installation image. For severe corruption, an in-place upgrade may be needed.

faq.q: How do I prevent component store corruption?
faq.a: Do not interrupt Windows updates in progress, avoid third-party cleanup tools that target WinSxS, and ensure your disk is healthy.

title: Update Installation Failed - Insufficient Space or VPN Connected
shortDescription: Windows Update failed to install because of insufficient System Reserved partition space or VPN interference.
fullDescription: Error 0x800f0922 typically indicates one of two problems: the System Reserved partition does not have enough free space to apply the update, or an active VPN connection is preventing the update from connecting to required servers. This is particularly common during feature updates.

causes.cause: System Reserved partition too small (less than 500MB)
Secure Boot or UEFI configuration issue

symptoms: Feature update fails during installation phase
Update rolls back after restart
Error appears after update downloads successfully
0x800f0922 in Windows Update history

fixSteps.title: Disconnect VPN
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect any active VPN connection before running Windows Update. This includes both app-based and system-level VPNs.

fixSteps.title: Check System Reserved partition
fixSteps.instruction: Open Disk Management (diskmgmt.msc). Check if the System Reserved or EFI System Partition has at least 500MB. If less, it may need to be expanded.

fixSteps.title: Expand System Reserved partition
fixSteps.instruction: Use a third-party partition manager (MiniTool Partition Wizard) to expand the System Reserved partition, or free space by removing extra language packs and fonts.

fixSteps.title: Repair .NET Framework
fixSteps.instruction: Open elevated Command Prompt: DISM /Online /Enable-Feature /FeatureName:NetFx3 /All. Also run the .NET Framework Repair Tool from Microsoft.

whenToCallPro: If the System Reserved partition needs to be expanded, this can be risky. Professional help ensures boot configuration is preserved.

faq.q: Why does VPN cause update failures?
faq.a: VPN connections can prevent Windows Update from reaching Microsoft's update verification servers, causing the installation to fail.

faq.q: How big should the System Reserved partition be?
faq.a: Microsoft recommends at least 500MB for UEFI systems. Some feature updates require even more space temporarily.

faq.q: Can I just skip this update?
faq.a: For feature updates, you can defer them. For security updates, try the fixes above to install them as soon as possible.

title: Source Files for Feature Not Found
shortDescription: Windows could not find the source files needed to install a Windows feature or update.
fullDescription: Error 0x800f081f (CBS_E_SOURCE_MISSING) occurs when DISM or Windows Update cannot find the source files needed to install a feature or repair components. This commonly happens when trying to enable .NET Framework 3.5, install optional features, or when DISM RestoreHealth cannot connect to Windows Update.

causes.cause: DISM unable to download source files from Windows Update
Group Policy blocking access to Windows Update for repair

symptoms: DISM /RestoreHealth fails with 0x800f081f
Enabling .NET Framework 3.5 fails
Optional Windows features fail to install
Windows Update fails to install cumulative updates

fixSteps.title: Use Windows installation media as source
fixSteps.instruction: Mount Windows ISO or insert USB. Run: DISM /Online /Enable-Feature /FeatureName:NetFx3 /All /Source:D:\Sources\sxs /LimitAccess

fixSteps.title: Check Group Policy
fixSteps.instruction: Open gpedit.msc > Computer Configuration > Administrative Templates > System > Specify settings for optional component installation and repair. Set to Enabled and check Contact Windows Update directly.

fixSteps.title: Clear WSUS setting in registry
fixSteps.instruction: Open Registry Editor: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsUpdate\AU. Set UseWUServer to 0, retry, then set it back.

fixSteps.title: Run DISM with alternative source
fixSteps.instruction: DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth /Source:D:\Sources\install.wim (where D: is your Windows media)

whenToCallPro: In enterprise environments where Group Policy and WSUS configuration requires IT administrator changes.

faq.q: Why can't DISM find the source files?
faq.a: DISM tries to download repair sources from Windows Update. If WU access is blocked by Group Policy, firewall, or WSUS settings, it cannot get the files.

faq.q: Do I need a Windows ISO?
faq.a: For .NET Framework 3.5 and some features, having Windows installation media as an alternative source is the most reliable fix.

faq.q: Does this affect managed/enterprise PCs more?
faq.a: Yes. Enterprise environments often use WSUS and Group Policies that restrict access to Windows Update, making this error much more common.

title: Update Prerequisite Missing
shortDescription: A prerequisite update required before this update could be installed is missing.
fullDescription: Error 0x800f0831 (CBS_E_STORE_CORRUPTION or prerequisite missing) occurs when a cumulative update requires a previous update that is not installed. This is common when updates are skipped or when the component store has gaps. It can also indicate delta update failures.
symptoms: Cumulative update fails to install
Update reverts after restart
Error appears only for specific KB updates
Update works on other machines with the same OS version

fixSteps.title: Install previous cumulative update first
fixSteps.instruction: Check the KB article for the failing update to identify prerequisites. Download and install the previous month's cumulative update from the Microsoft Update Catalog.

fixSteps.title: Run DISM and SFC
fixSteps.instruction: DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth then sfc /scannow

fixSteps.title: Clear update cache
fixSteps.instruction: Stop WU services, delete SoftwareDistribution folder contents, restart services.

fixSteps.title: Install full SSU and LCU
fixSteps.instruction: Download and install the latest Servicing Stack Update (SSU) first, then retry the cumulative update.

whenToCallPro: If the prerequisite chain cannot be determined or if multiple months of updates are missing.

faq.q: How do I find the prerequisite update?
faq.a: Check the Microsoft support page for the failing KB article. It will list prerequisites. Also try installing the previous month's cumulative update first.

faq.q: Can I skip directly to the latest update?
faq.a: Sometimes yes. Try downloading the full (non-delta) version of the latest cumulative update from the Microsoft Update Catalog.

faq.q: What is a Servicing Stack Update?
faq.a: An SSU updates the component that installs Windows updates. It must be current for cumulative updates to install correctly.

title: Element Not Found in Update Store
shortDescription: Windows Update could not find a required element in the update component store.
fullDescription: Error 0x80070490 (ERROR_NOT_FOUND) occurs when Windows Update's Component-Based Servicing (CBS) cannot find a required element in the component store. This indicates corruption in the update manifest or missing component registrations.

causes.cause: Corrupted CBS manifest or component registration

symptoms: Windows Update fails with 0x80070490
CBS log shows element not found errors
SFC finds unrepairable corruption
Specific updates repeatedly fail while others succeed

fixSteps.title: Run DISM CheckHealth and RestoreHealth
fixSteps.instruction: Open elevated Command Prompt: DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /CheckHealth then DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth

fixSteps.title: Run SFC
fixSteps.instruction: After DISM: sfc /scannow

fixSteps.title: Reset Windows Update components
fixSteps.instruction: Run the comprehensive Windows Update reset script that stops all services, clears caches, re-registers DLLs, and restarts services.

fixSteps.title: Manually install the update
fixSteps.instruction: Download the standalone update package from Microsoft Update Catalog and install it directly.

whenToCallPro: If DISM cannot repair the missing elements and manual installation also fails.

faq.q: What is CBS?
faq.a: Component-Based Servicing is the Windows subsystem responsible for installing, updating, and removing Windows components and updates.

faq.q: Can I check the CBS log?
faq.a: Yes. Open C:\Windows\Logs\CBS\CBS.log in Notepad. Search for the error code to find detailed information about what component is missing.

faq.q: Will an in-place upgrade fix this?
faq.a: Yes. An in-place upgrade using the Media Creation Tool replaces all components, which is the most thorough fix for persistent CBS errors.

title: Assembly Reference Mismatch
shortDescription: The referenced assembly is not installed on the system, preventing the update from applying.
fullDescription: Error 0x800736b3 (ERROR_SXS_ASSEMBLY_NOT_FOUND) occurs when Windows Update references a component assembly that is not installed or registered on the system. This is a side-by-side (SxS) assembly resolution failure.

causes.cause: Missing SxS assembly from component store
Failed previous update left inconsistent state

symptoms: Update fails with assembly reference error
DISM reports SxS assembly not found
Multiple updates fail with same error
SFC reports unrepairable files

fixSteps.title: Run DISM with source
fixSteps.instruction: Mount Windows ISO. Run: DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth /Source:D:\Sources\install.wim /LimitAccess

fixSteps.title: Run SFC
fixSteps.instruction: After DISM: sfc /scannow

fixSteps.title: Clean component store
fixSteps.instruction: DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /StartComponentCleanup /ResetBase

fixSteps.title: In-place upgrade
fixSteps.instruction: Use the Media Creation Tool to perform an in-place upgrade, which replaces all system assemblies.

whenToCallPro: If DISM with a clean source and in-place upgrade both fail to resolve the assembly mismatch.

faq.q: What is a SxS assembly?
faq.a: Side-by-side assemblies are Windows components stored in the WinSxS folder. They allow multiple versions of the same component to coexist safely.

faq.q: Can I copy assemblies from another PC?
faq.a: No. Manually copying WinSxS files can cause further corruption. Use DISM with a proper source image instead.

faq.q: Is an in-place upgrade safe?
faq.a: Yes. An in-place upgrade repairs Windows while preserving your files, applications, and settings. It is the most thorough repair option.

title: Windows Update Path Not Found
shortDescription: Windows Update could not find a required file path during the update process.
fullDescription: Error 0x80070003 (ERROR_PATH_NOT_FOUND) is closely related to 0x80070002 and occurs when Windows Update cannot locate a required directory or file path. The update cache may be corrupted or the path may have been modified.

causes.cause: Update cache pointing to non-existent paths

symptoms: Windows Update fails with 0x80070003
Update gets stuck at download or install phase
CBS log shows path not found errors
Windows Update Troubleshooter reports cache issue

fixSteps.title: Clear Windows Update cache
fixSteps.instruction: Stop WU services, rename SoftwareDistribution to SoftwareDistribution.old, restart services.

fixSteps.title: Run Windows Update Troubleshooter
fixSteps.instruction: Settings > Update & Security > Troubleshoot > Windows Update.

fixSteps.title: Run DISM and SFC
fixSteps.instruction: DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth then sfc /scannow

fixSteps.title: Re-register Windows Update DLLs
fixSteps.instruction: Open elevated Command Prompt and re-register WU DLLs: regsvr32 wuaueng.dll, regsvr32 wuapi.dll, regsvr32 wups.dll, etc.

whenToCallPro: If clearing the cache and re-registering DLLs do not resolve the path error.

faq.q: Can I just delete the SoftwareDistribution folder?
faq.a: Renaming it is safer than deleting. Windows will create a new one. If the rename works, you can delete the old folder after updates succeed.

faq.q: What is the SoftwareDistribution folder?
faq.a: It stores Windows Update files including downloaded updates, update history, and the update database. Recreating it forces a fresh start.

faq.q: Why does this keep recurring?
faq.a: Recurring failures may indicate a deeper system file issue. Run a full SFC and DISM repair cycle to address the root cause.

title: Windows Update Service Disabled
shortDescription: The Windows Update service is not running or has been disabled.
fullDescription: Error 0x80070422 occurs when the Windows Update service (wuauserv), BITS service, or other dependent services are stopped or disabled. This can happen when third-party optimization tools disable Windows services, when Group Policy restricts updates, or when malware disables the update mechanism.

causes.cause: Windows Update service disabled by optimization tool

symptoms: Windows Update fails immediately with 0x80070422
Windows Update says Service is not running
Cannot check for updates at all
Windows Firewall may also fail with same error

fixSteps.title: Enable Windows Update service
fixSteps.instruction: Open services.msc. Find Windows Update, double-click it, set Startup type to Automatic, click Start.

fixSteps.title: Enable BITS service
fixSteps.instruction: In services.msc, find Background Intelligent Transfer Service. Set to Automatic and Start.

fixSteps.title: Enable other dependent services
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure Cryptographic Services and Windows Installer services are also set to Automatic and running.

fixSteps.title: Check Group Policy
fixSteps.instruction: Open gpedit.msc > Computer Configuration > Administrative Templates > Windows Components > Windows Update. Ensure no policies are blocking updates.

whenToCallPro: If services keep disabling themselves after being started, malware may be responsible. Professional malware removal may be needed.

faq.q: Why was the service disabled?
faq.a: Third-party 'optimization' tools and some privacy tools disable Windows Update. Some malware also disables it to prevent security patches from being installed.

faq.q: What other services does Windows Update need?
faq.a: Windows Update depends on BITS, Cryptographic Services, Windows Installer, and Windows Event Log services. All must be running.

faq.q: Is it safe to disable Windows Update?
faq.a: No. Disabling Windows Update leaves your system vulnerable to security exploits. If you want to defer updates, use the built-in pause feature instead.

title: Windows Update Installation Error - MSI Failure
shortDescription: A Windows Installer (MSI) package failed during the update installation.
fullDescription: Error 0x80070643 (ERROR_INSTALL_FAILURE) occurs when a Windows Installer-based update package fails to install. This commonly affects .NET Framework updates, Windows Defender definition updates, and other MSI-based components. Corrupted .NET Framework, antivirus conflicts, or MSI service issues are typical causes.
symptoms: Specific updates fail with 0x80070643
.NET Framework updates consistently fail
Windows Defender definition updates fail
Update log shows MSI installation failure

fixSteps.title: Repair .NET Framework
fixSteps.instruction: Download and run the Microsoft .NET Framework Repair Tool. It automatically detects and fixes .NET issues.

fixSteps.title: Reset Windows Installer
fixSteps.instruction: Open elevated Command Prompt: msiexec /unregister then msiexec /regserver

fixSteps.title: Manually update Windows Defender
fixSteps.instruction: Open Windows Security > Virus & threat protection > Protection updates > Check for updates. Or download definitions from microsoft.com/wdsi.

fixSteps.title: Run DISM and SFC
fixSteps.instruction: DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth then sfc /scannow

whenToCallPro: If .NET Framework is severely corrupted and the repair tool cannot fix it.

faq.q: Why does this mainly affect .NET updates?
faq.a: .NET Framework updates use MSI packages. If the existing .NET installation is corrupted, new updates cannot apply their changes.

faq.q: Can I ignore Windows Defender definition failures?
faq.a: No. Defender definitions are critical for security. Update manually if automatic updates fail.

faq.q: What is MSI?
faq.a: MSI (Microsoft Installer) is the standard Windows installation technology. Many updates use MSI packages, which require the Windows Installer service.

title: Assembly Missing from Component Store
shortDescription: A required assembly is missing from the Windows component store.
fullDescription: Error 0x80073701 (ERROR_SXS_ASSEMBLY_MISSING) indicates that one or more component assemblies needed for the update are missing from the WinSxS store. This is similar to 0x800736b3 but indicates complete absence rather than reference mismatch.
symptoms: Cumulative updates fail with 0x80073701
DISM reports missing assemblies
SFC finds files it cannot repair
Error appears for feature updates

fixSteps.title: Run DISM RestoreHealth with source
fixSteps.instruction: DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth /Source:D:\Sources\install.wim (from Windows ISO)

fixSteps.title: Run SFC
fixSteps.instruction: sfc /scannow after DISM completes

fixSteps.title: Component cleanup
fixSteps.instruction: DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /StartComponentCleanup

fixSteps.title: In-place upgrade
fixSteps.instruction: Use Media Creation Tool to perform in-place upgrade repair.

whenToCallPro: If in-place upgrade fails to restore the missing assemblies.

faq.q: Can Disk Cleanup cause this?
faq.a: The built-in Disk Cleanup with Windows Update Cleanup option can sometimes cause this if it removes assemblies still needed by pending updates. This is uncommon but possible.

faq.q: Is an in-place upgrade different from a clean install?
faq.a: Yes. An in-place upgrade repairs Windows while keeping your files and applications. A clean install erases everything and starts fresh.

faq.q: How do I prevent assembly loss?
faq.a: Avoid third-party cleanup tools that target WinSxS. Do not interrupt Windows updates in progress. Run regular disk health checks.

title: Server Execution Failed
shortDescription: A Windows Update COM server execution failed.
fullDescription: Error 0x80080005 (CO_E_SERVER_EXEC_FAILURE) indicates that a COM server required by Windows Update failed to start or execute properly. This is typically related to the BITS or Windows Update Agent service failing to initialize.

causes.cause: Security software blocking COM server execution

symptoms: Windows Update fails with 0x80080005
BITS service crashes or fails to start
Windows Store also fails with same error
Update check fails immediately

fixSteps.title: Restart BITS and WU services
fixSteps.instruction: Open elevated Command Prompt: net stop bits && net stop wuauserv && net start bits && net start wuauserv

fixSteps.title: Re-register COM components
fixSteps.instruction: Run: regsvr32 qmgr.dll && regsvr32 qmgrprxy.dll

fixSteps.title: Reset Windows Update components
fixSteps.instruction: Run the full Windows Update reset procedure: stop services, clear caches, re-register DLLs, restart services.

fixSteps.title: Temporarily disable security software
fixSteps.instruction: Disable third-party antivirus and firewall temporarily, then retry the update.

whenToCallPro: If COM registration repairs do not work and the BITS service continues to fail.

faq.q: What is BITS?
faq.a: Background Intelligent Transfer Service handles asynchronous file downloads. Windows Update uses it to download update packages in the background.

faq.q: Can I reinstall BITS?
faq.a: BITS is a built-in Windows component and cannot be uninstalled/reinstalled. Re-registering its COM components (qmgr.dll) usually fixes initialization failures.

faq.q: Does this affect the Microsoft Store too?
faq.a: Yes. The Microsoft Store uses similar COM infrastructure, so this error can affect both Windows Update and Store app downloads.

title: Update Operation Cancelled
shortDescription: The Windows Update operation was cancelled before completion.
fullDescription: Error 0x8024000B (WU_E_CALL_CANCELLED) indicates that a Windows Update operation was cancelled before it could complete. This can happen when the user cancels an update, when Windows restarts during an update, or when a service timeout occurs.

causes.cause: Update cancelled by user or system restart
Service timeout during long update operation
Third-party software interfering with update process
System sleep or hibernate interrupting update

symptoms: Update shows as cancelled in update history
Updates start but never complete
Error appears after system restart during update
Updates seem to download but then show cancelled

fixSteps.title: Retry the update
fixSteps.instruction: Simply go to Windows Update and click Check for updates to retry. Ensure the PC stays on and connected.

fixSteps.title: Disable sleep during updates
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Power Options and set Sleep to Never while updates are installing.

fixSteps.title: Close background applications
fixSteps.instruction: Close all running applications before starting the update to prevent conflicts and timeouts.

fixSteps.title: Run update from clean boot
fixSteps.instruction: Perform a clean boot (msconfig > Services > Hide Microsoft services > Disable all) and then run Windows Update.

whenToCallPro: If updates are consistently cancelled despite keeping the system awake and online.

faq.q: Did I accidentally cancel the update?
faq.a: Possibly. Some actions like restarting the computer, closing the lid, or putting the PC to sleep during updates can cause cancellation.

faq.q: How long should I wait for updates?
faq.a: Most quality updates take 15-30 minutes. Feature updates can take 1-2 hours. Do not interrupt the process.

faq.q: Can my PC sleep during updates?
faq.a: No. Active Windows Update operations can be interrupted by sleep or hibernate. Disable these during update installation.

title: Windows Update Service Failed to Start
shortDescription: The Windows Update service failed to complete its startup properly.
fullDescription: Error 0x8024001E (WU_E_SERVICE_STOP) indicates that the Windows Update service stopped or failed during startup. This can be caused by corrupted service configuration, dependency failures, or resource exhaustion.
symptoms: Windows Update immediately fails with 0x8024001E
Windows Update service stops unexpectedly
Cannot check for updates
Event Viewer shows Windows Update service stop events

fixSteps.title: Start dependent services
fixSteps.instruction: Open services.msc. Start these services in order: Remote Procedure Call (RPC), DCOM Server Process Launcher, BITS, Cryptographic Services, Windows Update.

fixSteps.title: Reset Windows Update components
fixSteps.instruction: Stop all WU services, clear caches, re-register DLLs, restart services.

fixSteps.title: Run SFC and DISM
fixSteps.instruction: sfc /scannow then DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth

fixSteps.title: Check Event Viewer
fixSteps.instruction: Open Event Viewer > Windows Logs > System. Filter for Windows Update Client events to identify the specific failure reason.

whenToCallPro: If the service continues to stop after resetting components, professional registry or service repair may be needed.

faq.q: What services does Windows Update depend on?
faq.a: Key dependencies include BITS, Cryptographic Services, Remote Procedure Call (RPC), and DCOM Server Process Launcher.

faq.q: Can too many running programs cause this?
faq.a: If the system is extremely low on memory, services may fail to start. Close unnecessary applications and try again.

faq.q: How do I check if malware disabled the service?
faq.a: Run a full malware scan. Also check if the service startup type is set to Disabled in services.msc, which you did not set yourself.

title: Another Update Installation in Progress
shortDescription: Another installation is already in progress, preventing this update from installing.
fullDescription: Error 0x80240016 (WU_E_INSTALL_NOT_ALLOWED) occurs when Windows Update detects that another installation process is already running. Windows can only install one update at a time, and attempts to install a second update while one is in progress will fail.

causes.cause: Another Windows Update installation in progress

symptoms: Cannot install updates while another is in progress
Update appears stuck at a percentage
Multiple update attempts all fail with same error
Task Manager shows TiWorker.exe or wuauserv high activity

fixSteps.title: Wait for current installation to complete
fixSteps.instruction: Check Task Manager for TiWorker.exe or Windows Modules Installer Worker activity. Wait until it finishes.

fixSteps.title: Restart the computer
fixSteps.instruction: If the installation appears stuck, restart the computer. Pending updates will resume after reboot.

fixSteps.title: Stop Windows Update and retry
fixSteps.instruction: If stuck, open elevated Command Prompt: net stop wuauserv && net stop bits. Wait 30 seconds, then: net start wuauserv && net start bits

fixSteps.title: Clear pending installations
fixSteps.instruction: Delete contents of C:\Windows\SoftwareDistribution\Download. Restart Windows Update service.

whenToCallPro: If the installation is genuinely stuck and cannot be cleared by standard methods.

faq.q: How do I know if an update is stuck?
faq.a: If TiWorker.exe shows no CPU or disk activity for over 30 minutes but the update appears in progress, it may be stuck.

faq.q: Can I install two updates simultaneously?
faq.a: No. Windows installs updates sequentially. Attempting to force a second installation will result in this error.

faq.q: Is it safe to force-stop a stuck update?
faq.a: Yes, if the update has been stuck for over an hour with no activity. Stop the services, clear the cache, and restart.

title: Windows Update Handler Failure
shortDescription: The Windows Update handler encountered an unexpected failure during processing.
fullDescription: Error 0x80242006 (WU_E_UH_INVALIDMETADATA) indicates that the update handler received invalid metadata from the update package. This can occur due to corrupted update downloads, proxy server caching issues, or WSUS synchronization problems.

causes.cause: Proxy server serving stale cached content

symptoms: Update fails during installation with metadata error
Specific updates fail while others succeed
Error appears consistently for same update
Update handler shows failure in CBS log

fixSteps.title: Clear update cache
fixSteps.instruction: Stop WU services, delete C:\Windows\SoftwareDistribution\Download contents, restart services.

fixSteps.title: Run Windows Update Troubleshooter
fixSteps.instruction: Settings > Update & Security > Troubleshoot > Windows Update.

fixSteps.title: Download update manually
fixSteps.instruction: Get the standalone update from catalog.update.microsoft.com.

fixSteps.title: Bypass proxy for Windows Update
fixSteps.instruction: Configure proxy exclusions for *.windowsupdate.com and *.microsoft.com.

whenToCallPro: In enterprise environments where WSUS or proxy configuration needs IT admin changes.

faq.q: What is update metadata?
faq.a: Metadata describes the update package, its dependencies, applicability, and installation instructions. If corrupted, Windows cannot process the update correctly.

faq.q: Can a proxy cause this error?
faq.a: Yes. Caching proxy servers may serve outdated metadata. Bypassing the proxy for Windows Update URLs often fixes this.

faq.q: Does this affect all updates?
faq.a: Usually only specific updates with corrupted cached metadata. Clearing the cache and re-downloading typically resolves it.

title: Windows Update HTTP 403 Forbidden
shortDescription: Access to Windows Update servers was blocked with an HTTP 403 response.
fullDescription: Error 0x80244018 (WU_E_PT_HTTP_STATUS_FORBIDDEN) indicates that a firewall, proxy, or content filter is blocking access to Windows Update servers with a 403 Forbidden response. This is common in enterprise and school networks.

causes.cause: Corporate firewall blocking Windows Update URLs

symptoms: Cannot check for or download updates
Error 0x80244018 in update history
Updates work on other networks but not current one
Store app downloads also fail

fixSteps.title: Try a different network
fixSteps.instruction: Connect to a different network (mobile hotspot, home network) to verify if it is a network-level block.

fixSteps.title: Configure proxy exclusions
fixSteps.instruction: Add *.windowsupdate.com, *.microsoft.com, and *.windows.com to proxy bypass list.

fixSteps.title: Reset network settings
fixSteps.instruction: netsh winsock reset && netsh int ip reset && ipconfig /flushdns

fixSteps.title: Manual update installation
fixSteps.instruction: Download updates from catalog.update.microsoft.com on a different network and transfer via USB.

whenToCallPro: If on a corporate network, contact your IT department to allow Windows Update through the firewall.

faq.q: Why is my network blocking Windows Update?
faq.a: Some corporate and school networks restrict Windows Update to force updates through a managed server (WSUS/SCCM). Contact IT for assistance.

faq.q: Can I use a VPN to bypass the block?
faq.a: A VPN can bypass network-level blocks, but this may violate network policies. Check with your IT department first.

faq.q: What URLs need to be unblocked?
faq.a: Key domains include windowsupdate.com, update.microsoft.com, download.windowsupdate.com, and related CDN domains.

title: Update Download Incomplete
shortDescription: The update download did not complete because the download manager could not map the file.
fullDescription: Error 0x80246007 (WU_E_DM_NOTDOWNLOADED) occurs when BITS fails to complete the update download. The download manager could not finalize the downloaded file, typically due to disk space issues, corrupted BITS queue, or network interruptions.
symptoms: Download progress shows then fails
Update stuck at a download percentage
Error 0x80246007 in update history
BITS queue shows failed downloads

fixSteps.title: Free disk space
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure at least 10GB free on C: drive. Use Disk Cleanup to remove temporary files, previous Windows installations, and update cache.

fixSteps.title: Reset BITS queue
fixSteps.instruction: Open elevated Command Prompt: bitsadmin /reset /allusers

fixSteps.title: Clear download cache
fixSteps.instruction: Stop WU and BITS services. Delete contents of C:\Windows\SoftwareDistribution\Download. Restart services.

fixSteps.title: Manual download
fixSteps.instruction: Download the update directly from Microsoft Update Catalog.

whenToCallPro: If the BITS queue is consistently corrupted and manual methods do not resolve it.

faq.q: How much disk space do updates need?
faq.a: Quality updates need 2-5GB free. Feature updates can require 10-20GB or more. Always maintain adequate free space on the system drive.

faq.q: What is BITS queue?
faq.a: BITS manages download jobs in a queue. Corrupted queue entries can prevent new downloads from completing. Resetting the queue clears stuck entries.

faq.q: Can a network interruption cause this?
faq.a: Yes. If the network disconnects during download, the file may be incomplete. BITS normally resumes, but corruption can prevent this.

title: Update Metadata Integrity Verification Failed
shortDescription: Windows Update failed to verify the integrity of update metadata.
fullDescription: Error 0x80248007 (WU_E_DS_NODATA) indicates that the Windows Update data store does not contain the expected metadata for the update. This can occur when the update database is corrupted or when metadata integrity checks fail.

causes.cause: Clock/time synchronization issue affecting cert validation

symptoms: Cannot check for updates
Updates fail with integrity error
Windows Update database seems empty or corrupted
Checking for updates returns no results

fixSteps.title: Sync system clock
fixSteps.instruction: Right-click clock > Adjust date/time > Sync now. Ensure time zone is correct.

fixSteps.title: Reset Windows Update database
fixSteps.instruction: Stop WU services. Rename C:\Windows\SoftwareDistribution to SoftwareDistribution.old. Restart services.

fixSteps.title: Re-register update DLLs
fixSteps.instruction: Run: regsvr32 wuaueng.dll && regsvr32 wuapi.dll && regsvr32 wups.dll && regsvr32 wups2.dll

fixSteps.title: Run Windows Update Troubleshooter
fixSteps.instruction: Settings > Troubleshoot > Windows Update.

whenToCallPro: If metadata integrity failures persist after database reset, there may be security concerns requiring investigation.

faq.q: Can an incorrect system clock cause this?
faq.a: Yes. Certificate validation and metadata signing rely on correct system time. An incorrect clock can cause integrity checks to fail.

faq.q: Is this a security concern?
faq.a: Usually not - it is most often caused by database corruption. However, if it occurs alongside other suspicious activity, investigation may be warranted.

faq.q: How do I reset the update database safely?
faq.a: Renaming the SoftwareDistribution folder is safe. Windows creates a new one automatically with a fresh database.

title: Windows Update Restart Required
shortDescription: A system restart is required to complete the update, but the restart was not performed.
fullDescription: Error 0x8024A105 (WU_E_AU_OOBE_IN_PROGRESS or restart needed) indicates that Windows Update cannot proceed because a pending restart is required. Previous updates need to be finalized through a restart before new updates can be installed.

causes.cause: Pending restart from previous update not completed
System stuck in pending restart state

symptoms: Cannot install new updates
Windows shows restart pending notification
Update history shows waiting for restart
Multiple updates queued behind restart

fixSteps.title: Restart the computer
fixSteps.instruction: Save all work and restart the computer. Do not use Shut down; use Restart to ensure pending updates are applied.

fixSteps.title: Clear pending restart flag
fixSteps.instruction: If restart does not clear the error, check registry: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\WindowsUpdate\Auto Update\RebootRequired. Delete this key.

fixSteps.title: Run Windows Update Troubleshooter
fixSteps.instruction: Settings > Troubleshoot > Windows Update.

fixSteps.title: Clear pending operations
fixSteps.instruction: Delete C:\Windows\WinSxS\pending.xml if it exists (boot from recovery to access this file if Windows is stuck).

whenToCallPro: If the system is stuck in a restart loop and cannot complete the pending updates.

faq.q: Why does Restart work but Shut down does not?
faq.a: Windows 10/11 uses Fast Startup which does not fully shut down. Restart performs a full shutdown and restart, allowing pending updates to finalize.

faq.q: Can I defer the restart?
faq.a: Yes, but deferred restarts prevent new updates from installing. Schedule the restart for a convenient time using Active hours.

faq.q: What if my PC is stuck restarting?
faq.a: If the PC keeps restarting and applying updates, let it complete. If it loops for more than 3 restarts, it may need recovery mode intervention.

title: Windows Update Proxy Authentication Required
shortDescription: The proxy server requires authentication that was not provided.
fullDescription: Error 0x8024402C (WU_E_PT_WINHTTP_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED or proxy auth needed) indicates that Windows Update cannot connect because a proxy server requires authentication credentials. This is common in enterprise environments with authenticated proxy servers.

causes.cause: Proxy requiring authentication not configured for WU
DNS resolution failure for update servers

symptoms: Cannot check for or download updates
Error occurs only on corporate/enterprise network
Updates work on home network but not office
Browser can access internet but WU cannot

fixSteps.title: Configure WinHTTP proxy
fixSteps.instruction: Open elevated Command Prompt: netsh winhttp set proxy proxy-server="proxyserver:port" bypass-list="*.local"

fixSteps.title: Import browser proxy settings
fixSteps.instruction: Run: netsh winhttp import proxy source=ie to import proxy settings from Internet Explorer/Edge.

fixSteps.title: Reset WinHTTP proxy
fixSteps.instruction: If proxy is not needed: netsh winhttp reset proxy

fixSteps.title: Flush DNS and reset network
fixSteps.instruction: ipconfig /flushdns && netsh winsock reset && netsh int ip reset

whenToCallPro: Contact IT support for proper proxy configuration in enterprise environments.

faq.q: Why does my browser work but Windows Update does not?
faq.a: Browsers use their own proxy settings, while Windows Update uses WinHTTP proxy settings which are configured separately.

faq.q: How do I find my proxy settings?
faq.a: Check with IT, or look in browser settings. Also run: netsh winhttp show proxy to see current WinHTTP settings.

faq.q: Can I bypass the proxy for Windows Update?
faq.a: In some cases, yes. Add Windows Update URLs to the proxy bypass list if your network allows direct connections.

title: Feature Update Driver Compatibility Error
shortDescription: A driver compatibility issue prevented a Windows feature update from completing.
fullDescription: Error 0xC1900101 is a generic rollback code for Windows feature updates (version upgrades). It indicates that a driver caused the upgrade to fail during one of its phases. The sub-code (e.g., 0xC1900101 - 0x20017) identifies which phase failed. This is the most common feature update failure.
symptoms: Feature update rolls back after reaching a percentage
Error code 0xC1900101 with various sub-codes
Update process starts but reverts to previous version
Computer restarts multiple times then rolls back

fixSteps.title: Update all device drivers
fixSteps.instruction: Update all drivers, especially GPU, network, storage, and chipset drivers. Download from manufacturer websites, not Windows Update.

fixSteps.title: Disconnect non-essential peripherals
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect all USB devices, external displays, printers, and docking stations. Keep only keyboard and mouse.

fixSteps.title: Uninstall third-party antivirus
fixSteps.instruction: Fully uninstall third-party antivirus software before the feature update. Reinstall after successful upgrade.

fixSteps.title: Free disk space
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure at least 20GB free on C: drive. Run Disk Cleanup with system file cleanup option.

fixSteps.title: Update BIOS
fixSteps.instruction: Check for and install BIOS updates from your computer or motherboard manufacturer.

whenToCallPro: If the feature update continues to fail after all driver updates and peripheral disconnection.

faq.q: What do the sub-codes mean?
faq.a: The sub-code indicates which upgrade phase failed. For example, 0x20017 means failure during the first boot phase, 0x30018 means during the second boot phase.

faq.q: Can I skip this feature update?
faq.a: You can defer feature updates, but they will eventually be required. It is better to fix the driver issue.

faq.q: Should I try a clean install instead?
faq.a: As a last resort, yes. A clean install avoids driver compatibility issues since all drivers are installed fresh.

title: System Does Not Meet Requirements for Update
shortDescription: The computer does not meet the minimum requirements for the Windows feature update.
fullDescription: Error 0xC1900200 indicates that the system does not meet the minimum hardware or software requirements for the target Windows version. This became very common with the Windows 11 upgrade due to its TPM 2.0, Secure Boot, and CPU requirements.

causes.cause: CPU not on supported processor list (Windows 11)
TPM 2.0 not present or not enabled

symptoms: Feature update blocked with requirements not met
PC Health Check shows incompatibility
Windows Update says this PC does not meet requirements
Setup wizard shows system requirements error

fixSteps.title: Run PC Health Check
fixSteps.instruction: Download and run the PC Health Check app from Microsoft to identify which requirements are not met.

fixSteps.title: Enable TPM in BIOS
fixSteps.instruction: Enter BIOS > Security > TPM. Enable Intel PTT or AMD fTPM. This enables the firmware TPM.

fixSteps.title: Enable Secure Boot
fixSteps.instruction: Enter BIOS > Boot > Secure Boot. Set to Enabled. Note: disk may need to be GPT format.

fixSteps.title: Check CPU compatibility
fixSteps.instruction: Check Microsoft's supported processor list for Windows 11. If your CPU is not listed, you may need to use the registry bypass or remain on Windows 10.

whenToCallPro: If hardware upgrades (RAM, TPM module) are needed and you are not comfortable with hardware installation.

faq.q: Can I install Windows 11 on unsupported hardware?
faq.a: Microsoft offers a registry bypass for TPM and CPU requirements, but warns that unsupported PCs may not receive future updates.

faq.q: Do I need a discrete TPM chip?
faq.a: No. Most modern CPUs have firmware TPM (Intel PTT, AMD fTPM) built in. You just need to enable it in BIOS.

faq.q: What if my CPU is not supported?
faq.a: You can continue using Windows 10 (supported until October 2025) or upgrade your hardware. Some users install Windows 11 using the registry bypass at their own risk.

title: Incompatible Application Blocking Update
shortDescription: An installed application is incompatible with the target Windows version and is blocking the update.
fullDescription: Error 0xC1900208 indicates that one or more installed applications are known to be incompatible with the target Windows version. Windows blocks the update to prevent application issues. The setup log identifies the specific blocking application(s).

causes.cause: Incompatible application on Microsoft's block list

symptoms: Feature update blocked with compatibility warning
Setup mentions specific application as blocker
Update refuses to start installation
Compatibility Appraiser shows blocking issues

fixSteps.title: Identify the blocking application
fixSteps.instruction: Check the setup log at C:\$WINDOWS.~BT\Sources\Panther\compat*.xml or run the Windows Setup directly to see which app is flagged.

fixSteps.title: Update or uninstall the blocking application
fixSteps.instruction: Update the application to its latest version. If no compatible version exists, uninstall it before the update.

fixSteps.title: Retry the feature update
fixSteps.instruction: After removing the blocker, go to Windows Update and retry the feature update.

fixSteps.title: Reinstall application after update
fixSteps.instruction: After the feature update succeeds, reinstall the application. The latest version may now be compatible.

whenToCallPro: If the blocking application is critical for work and no compatible version is available.

faq.q: Will I lose the application if I uninstall it?
faq.a: You can reinstall it after the update. Back up application data and license keys before uninstalling.

faq.q: Can I force the update to ignore the block?
faq.a: Not through normal Windows Update. You can try using the Media Creation Tool with a manual installation, but the application may not work correctly after the update.

faq.q: How does Microsoft decide which apps to block?
faq.a: Microsoft maintains a compatibility database of applications known to cause issues with new Windows versions. This database is updated regularly.

title: Feature Update Rolled Back During Migration
shortDescription: The feature update failed during the migration phase and rolled back to the previous version.
fullDescription: Error 0xC190020E indicates that the Windows feature update failed during the migration phase (transferring data and settings from the old version to the new). This can be caused by corrupted user profiles, application data issues, or filesystem problems.

causes.cause: Application data incompatible with new version

symptoms: Feature update progresses then rolls back
Rollback occurs during migration or OOBE phase
Multiple upgrade attempts all fail at same point
SetupDiag shows migration failure

fixSteps.title: Run SetupDiag
fixSteps.instruction: Download Microsoft SetupDiag tool. Run it to identify the specific migration failure cause.

fixSteps.title: Free disk space
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure at least 20GB free on C: drive for the migration phase.

fixSteps.title: Run CHKDSK
fixSteps.instruction: chkdsk C: /f /r to repair any filesystem errors.

fixSteps.title: Create new user profile and migrate
fixSteps.instruction: Create a new local admin account, copy data, and try the update from the new profile. This bypasses corrupted profile data.

whenToCallPro: If the migration continues to fail after disk repairs and profile recreation.

faq.q: Will I lose data if the update rolls back?
faq.a: No. When a feature update rolls back, your previous Windows version is restored with all data intact.

faq.q: What is SetupDiag?
faq.a: SetupDiag is a Microsoft tool that analyzes Windows Setup log files to determine why an upgrade failed. It provides specific failure reasons and remediation steps.

faq.q: Should I try a clean install?
faq.a: If migration repeatedly fails, a clean install may be the most reliable option. Back up all data first.

title: Insufficient Disk Space for Update
shortDescription: There is not enough disk space to install the Windows update.
fullDescription: Error 0x80070070 (ERROR_DISK_FULL) is one of the most straightforward update errors - the system drive does not have enough free space to download, extract, and install the update. Quality updates need several gigabytes, and feature updates can require 20GB or more.

causes.cause: System drive is full or nearly full
Large temporary files consuming disk space
Previous Windows installation taking up space
System drive is too small for current Windows version

symptoms: Update fails with 0x80070070 or disk full error
C: drive shows very low free space
Feature update fails during extraction phase
Download succeeds but installation fails

fixSteps.title: Run Disk Cleanup with system files
fixSteps.instruction: Open Disk Cleanup, click Clean up system files. Select Windows Update Cleanup, Previous Windows installations, and Temporary files.

fixSteps.title: Clear temp files
fixSteps.instruction: Open Run (Win+R), type %temp%, delete all files in the folder. Also type temp and delete contents.

fixSteps.title: Move files to external storage
fixSteps.instruction: Move large files (Downloads, Videos, Documents) to an external drive or cloud storage.

fixSteps.title: Use external storage for update
fixSteps.instruction: For feature updates, Windows can use a USB drive as temporary storage. Plug in an empty 16GB+ USB drive and retry the update.

whenToCallPro: If the system drive is permanently too small and needs to be upgraded to a larger one.

faq.q: How much space do I need?
faq.a: Quality updates need about 5-10GB free. Feature updates need at least 20GB free. Maintaining 15-20% free space on C: is recommended.

faq.q: Can I use an external drive for the update?
faq.a: Yes. Windows 10 and later can use an external USB drive as temporary storage during feature updates. The drive needs to be at least 16GB.

faq.q: What takes up so much space?
faq.a: Common space consumers include previous Windows installations (Windows.old), Windows Update cache, temporary files, and the hibernation file.

title: Windows Update Transaction Error
shortDescription: A transaction error occurred during Windows Update installation.
fullDescription: Error 0x80070BC9 (ERROR_FAIL_NOACTION_REBOOT) occurs when the Windows servicing infrastructure encounters a transaction error. This typically means a previous update is still pending completion or the servicing stack has pending transactions that need to be resolved.

causes.cause: Pending servicing transactions from previous update

symptoms: Update installation fails with transaction error
Multiple restarts do not clear the pending state
DISM reports pending operations
CBS log shows transaction rollback

fixSteps.title: Restart the computer
fixSteps.instruction: Restart (not shut down) the computer to complete any pending transactions.

fixSteps.title: Clear pending transactions
fixSteps.instruction: Open elevated Command Prompt: DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RevertPendingActions

fixSteps.title: Run SFC and DISM
fixSteps.instruction: sfc /scannow then DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth

fixSteps.title: Reset servicing stack
fixSteps.instruction: Install the latest Servicing Stack Update manually from the Microsoft Update Catalog.

whenToCallPro: If pending transactions cannot be cleared and the servicing stack is in a broken state.

faq.q: What is a servicing transaction?
faq.a: Windows uses transactional updates to ensure they can be rolled back if something goes wrong. Pending transactions are incomplete update operations.

faq.q: Can I force-clear pending operations?
faq.a: DISM /RevertPendingActions is the safest way to clear pending operations. It cancels any queued changes that have not been committed.

faq.q: Will clearing pending operations lose my updates?
faq.a: It will revert any pending (not yet committed) update changes. Successfully installed updates are not affected.

title: Unspecified Windows Update Error
shortDescription: An unspecified error occurred in Windows Update.
fullDescription: Error 0x80240FFF (WU_E_UNEXPECTED) is a catch-all error code that does not map to a specific failure condition. It is commonly seen after a fresh Windows installation or when Windows Update's internal state is inconsistent. The error often resolves on its own after waiting or performing basic troubleshooting.

causes.cause: Windows Update service in inconsistent state
Fresh Windows installation not yet synced

symptoms: Generic error when checking for updates
No specific error details provided
Error appears after clean Windows installation
Updates worked before but suddenly fail

fixSteps.title: Wait and retry
fixSteps.instruction: Wait 15-30 minutes and try checking for updates again. This error often resolves on its own.

fixSteps.title: Run Windows Update Troubleshooter
fixSteps.instruction: Settings > Troubleshoot > Windows Update.

fixSteps.title: Reset Windows Update components
fixSteps.instruction: Stop WU services, clear SoftwareDistribution and catroot2, restart services.

fixSteps.title: Run SFC and DISM
fixSteps.instruction: sfc /scannow then DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after multiple days and all standard troubleshooting steps have been exhausted.

faq.q: Why is this error so vague?
faq.a: 0x80240FFF is Windows Update's generic error code used when the specific failure does not match any defined error. It often indicates a transient issue.

faq.q: Does a fresh install need time to sync?
faq.a: Yes. After a fresh Windows installation, Windows Update may need time to synchronize its database. Waiting a few hours and retrying often works.

faq.q: Should I worry about this error?
faq.a: If it is a one-time occurrence, no. If it persists for multiple days, use the troubleshooting steps above to resolve it.

title: Update Certificate Chain Validation Failed
shortDescription: The certificate chain used to sign the update could not be validated.
fullDescription: Error 0x800B0109 (CERT_E_UNTRUSTEDROOT) occurs when Windows cannot validate the digital signature of an update package because the signing certificate chain is not trusted. This can happen due to missing root certificates, incorrect system time, or certificate store corruption.

causes.cause: Incorrect system date/time affecting certificate validation

symptoms: All updates fail with certificate error
Windows Update says signature verification failed
Manual update installation also fails
Error occurs after date/time change

fixSteps.title: Fix system date and time
fixSteps.instruction: Right-click the clock > Adjust date/time. Ensure Set time automatically is on. Click Sync now.

fixSteps.title: Install root certificate updates
fixSteps.instruction: Download and install the latest Microsoft Root Certificate Update from the Microsoft Update Catalog.

fixSteps.title: Reset Cryptographic Services
fixSteps.instruction: Open services.msc, restart the Cryptographic Services service.

fixSteps.title: Check for proxy interference
fixSteps.instruction: If behind a corporate proxy, verify it is not performing SSL inspection that breaks Windows Update certificate chains.

whenToCallPro: If certificate issues persist after time correction and root certificate installation, there may be security concerns.

faq.q: Why does system time matter for certificates?
faq.a: Digital certificates have validity periods. If your system clock is wrong, a valid certificate may appear expired or not-yet-valid, causing validation failure.

faq.q: Can a dead CMOS battery cause this?
faq.a: Yes. A dead CMOS battery causes the system clock to reset on each boot, which can invalidate certificate timestamps. Replace the battery.

faq.q: Is this a security threat?
faq.a: Usually not - it is typically a time sync or certificate cache issue. However, if all other causes are ruled out, a man-in-the-middle attack should be investigated.

title: Delivery Optimization Download Failure
shortDescription: Windows Delivery Optimization failed to download update content.
fullDescription: Error 0x80D02002 indicates a failure in Windows Delivery Optimization (DO), the peer-to-peer content delivery system used by Windows Update. This can occur when DO cannot download content from peers or Microsoft CDN servers.
symptoms: Update download fails with DO error
Store app downloads also fail
Downloads are extremely slow
DO monitor shows 0% download progress

fixSteps.title: Restart Delivery Optimization service
fixSteps.instruction: Open services.msc, find Delivery Optimization, restart it. Ensure startup type is Automatic.

fixSteps.title: Clear DO cache
fixSteps.instruction: Open elevated Command Prompt: net stop dosvc && del /q C:\Windows\ServiceProfiles\NetworkService\AppData\Local\Microsoft\Windows\DeliveryOptimization\Cache\* && net start dosvc

fixSteps.title: Check DO settings
fixSteps.instruction: Settings > Update & Security > Delivery Optimization. Ensure Allow downloads from other PCs is enabled.

fixSteps.title: Disable DO temporarily
fixSteps.instruction: If DO continues to fail, disable it in Settings and rely on direct download from Microsoft servers.

whenToCallPro: In enterprise environments where DO is configured through Group Policy and needs IT admin changes.

faq.q: What is Delivery Optimization?
faq.a: DO is a Windows peer-to-peer content delivery system that downloads updates from both Microsoft servers and other PCs on your network to speed up downloads.

faq.q: Can I disable it?
faq.a: Yes. Disabling DO forces all downloads directly from Microsoft servers. This may be slower but can resolve DO-specific errors.

faq.q: Does DO use my bandwidth to upload to others?
faq.a: By default, yes. DO may share downloaded update content with other PCs. You can limit this or disable it in Settings > Delivery Optimization.

title: Update Requires Restart to Complete
shortDescription: The update was partially installed and requires a system restart to complete.
fullDescription: Error 0x80070BC2 (ERROR_SUCCESS_REBOOT_REQUIRED) actually indicates a partial success - the update has been staged and requires a system restart to complete installation. However, if the restart was interrupted or failed, this code persists as an error.

causes.cause: System restart pending for staged update
Pending restart from another update blocking this one

symptoms: Update shows as pending restart
New updates cannot install until restart is done
System shows update pending for extended period
Restart does not seem to complete the update

fixSteps.title: Restart the computer
fixSteps.instruction: Use Start > Restart (not Shut down) to properly restart and apply pending updates.

fixSteps.title: Check for stuck pending state
fixSteps.instruction: After restart, if still pending, check registry key: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Component Based Servicing\RebootPending

fixSteps.title: Run DISM cleanup
fixSteps.instruction: DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /StartComponentCleanup

fixSteps.title: Clear pending operations if stuck
fixSteps.instruction: If restart does not help: DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RevertPendingActions

whenToCallPro: If the system is stuck in a pending restart state that cannot be cleared.

faq.q: Is this actually an error?
faq.a: Not exactly. It means the update was successfully staged but needs a restart to finalize. A simple restart usually resolves it.

faq.q: What if the restart does not work?
faq.a: If the update does not complete after restart, the staging may have been corrupted. Use DISM /RevertPendingActions and retry the update.

faq.q: Why does Shut down not work the same as Restart?
faq.a: Windows 10/11 uses Fast Startup with Shut down, which does not fully restart the system. Restart performs a full shutdown and restart, allowing pending updates to apply.

title: Windows Update Error - Out of Memory
shortDescription: Windows Update ran out of memory while attempting to process the update.
fullDescription:
Error 0x8007000E (E_OUTOFMEMORY) occurs when the Windows Update client or its associated services cannot allocate enough memory to complete the update operation. This is frequently caused by insufficient system RAM, excessive background processes consuming memory, or a memory leak in the update agent itself.

The error is most common on systems with 4 GB of RAM or less, particularly when a large cumulative update is being installed. Memory-intensive applications running alongside Windows Update can also trigger this condition. In some cases, the Windows Update cache has grown extremely large and the service cannot load all necessary metadata into memory at once.

Closing unnecessary applications, increasing virtual memory, and clearing the update cache are the primary remediation steps. If the issue persists on a system with adequate RAM, it may indicate a deeper problem with the update components or a memory leak that requires service restart.

causes.cause: Insufficient physical RAM for update processing
Too many background applications consuming memory
Bloated Windows Update cache consuming excessive memory
Memory leak in Windows Update service

symptoms: Windows Update fails with error 0x8007000E
System becomes sluggish during update attempts
Update downloads partially then fails
Task Manager shows high memory usage during updates

fixSteps.title: Close unnecessary applications
fixSteps.instruction: Open Task Manager (Ctrl+Shift+Esc), go to the Processes tab, and close any non-essential applications to free memory before retrying the update.

fixSteps.title: Increase virtual memory
fixSteps.instruction: Go to System Properties > Advanced > Performance Settings > Advanced > Virtual Memory > Change. Uncheck 'Automatically manage' and set the paging file to 1.5x your physical RAM.

fixSteps.title: Clear Windows Update cache
fixSteps.instruction: Open elevated Command Prompt. Run: net stop wuauserv && net stop bits && rd /s /q C:\Windows\SoftwareDistribution && net start wuauserv && net start bits

fixSteps.title: Restart Windows Update service
fixSteps.instruction: Open Services (services.msc), locate Windows Update, right-click and select Restart. This clears any memory leaks in the service.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after clearing the cache and increasing virtual memory, or if your system consistently runs out of memory during updates despite having adequate RAM.

faq.q: How much RAM do I need for Windows Update?
faq.a: Microsoft recommends at least 4 GB for Windows 10/11, but large cumulative updates can require more available memory. Having 8 GB or more significantly reduces update-related memory issues.

faq.q: Will adding more RAM fix this permanently?
faq.a: In most cases, yes. If your system has only 4 GB, upgrading to 8 GB or more will prevent most memory-related update failures.

title: Windows Update Error - Sharing Violation
shortDescription: A file needed by Windows Update is locked by another process.
fullDescription:
Error 0x80070020 (ERROR_SHARING_VIOLATION) occurs when Windows Update cannot access a required file because it is locked or in use by another process. This typically happens when antivirus software is scanning update files, a third-party program has a handle on a system file, or another instance of Windows Update is already running.

The most common culprit is real-time antivirus scanning. When Windows Update downloads files, the antivirus may immediately lock them for scanning, preventing the update service from processing them. Backup software, disk defragmenters, and other system utilities that access system files can also trigger this error.

Temporarily disabling real-time antivirus protection, performing a clean boot to eliminate third-party interference, or using the Windows Update troubleshooter are the recommended approaches to resolve this sharing violation.

causes.cause: Antivirus software locking update files during scan
Third-party application holding a lock on system files
Another Windows Update instance or installer running simultaneously
Backup software accessing system files during update

symptoms: Windows Update fails with error 0x80070020
Update installation fails at a specific percentage
Error appears immediately when update tries to install
Multiple retries fail with the same error

fixSteps.title: Temporarily disable antivirus real-time protection
fixSteps.instruction: Open your antivirus software and temporarily disable real-time scanning. For Windows Defender: Settings > Privacy & Security > Windows Security > Virus & threat protection > Manage settings > toggle off Real-time protection. Retry the update.

fixSteps.title: Perform a clean boot
fixSteps.instruction: Run msconfig, go to Services tab, check 'Hide all Microsoft services', click 'Disable all'. Go to Startup tab, open Task Manager, disable all startup items. Restart and retry the update.

fixSteps.title: Identify the locking process
fixSteps.instruction: Download Process Explorer from Microsoft Sysinternals. Use Find > Find Handle or DLL, search for the file mentioned in the update log (C:\Windows\Logs\CBS\CBS.log) to identify which process holds the lock.

fixSteps.title: Run Windows Update in Safe Mode
fixSteps.instruction: Restart into Safe Mode with Networking (hold Shift while clicking Restart > Troubleshoot > Advanced > Startup Settings). Run Windows Update from Safe Mode.

whenToCallPro: If a clean boot does not resolve the issue and you cannot identify which process is locking the files.

faq.q: Is it safe to disable antivirus for updates?
faq.a: Yes, temporarily disabling real-time scanning while installing Windows updates is safe. Windows Defender will automatically re-enable after a period, and the updates themselves are cryptographically signed.

faq.q: What is a sharing violation?
faq.a: A sharing violation means two programs are trying to use the same file simultaneously and one has exclusive access. The second program (in this case Windows Update) is denied access.

title: Windows Update Error - Request Not Supported
shortDescription: The update request is not supported by the current system configuration.
fullDescription:
Error 0x80070032 (ERROR_NOT_SUPPORTED) occurs when Windows Update attempts an operation that the current system configuration does not support. This frequently happens when update delivery optimization settings conflict with network policies, when the system is running an edition that does not support a specific update feature, or when Group Policy restrictions block the requested update method.

This error is particularly common in enterprise environments where administrators have configured restrictive Windows Update policies. It can also appear when Delivery Optimization is misconfigured or when the system is attempting to use a peer-to-peer update method that is blocked by the network.

Reviewing and adjusting Delivery Optimization settings, checking Group Policy for restrictive update policies, and resetting Windows Update components are the primary resolution paths.

causes.cause: Unsupported update feature for the Windows edition

symptoms: Windows Update fails with error 0x80070032
Updates fail to download but check succeeds
Delivery Optimization related errors in event log
Update works on other machines but not this one

fixSteps.title: Reset Delivery Optimization settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Windows Update > Advanced options > Delivery Optimization. Toggle off 'Allow downloads from other PCs'. Wait 30 seconds, toggle it back on if desired. Retry the update.

fixSteps.title: Check Group Policy for update restrictions
fixSteps.instruction: Run gpedit.msc, navigate to Computer Configuration > Administrative Templates > Windows Components > Windows Update. Ensure no policies are blocking the update method. Set any conflicting policies to 'Not Configured'.

fixSteps.title: Reset Windows Update components
fixSteps.instruction: Open elevated Command Prompt.
